titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Brooklyn newsletter collection ARC.174
titlestmt.author: Weatherly Stephan
titlestmt.sponsor: This collection was originally processed and described as part of the project, "Uncovering the Secrets of Brooklyn's 19th Century Past: Creation to Consolidation," funded by the Council on Library and Information Resources, with additional support from The Gladys Krieble Delmas Foundation.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-05 18:50:20 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

physdesc.extent: in four document boxes and one flat box

did.abstract: A composite collection of newsletters from Brooklyn-based businesses and organizations.
did.langmaterial: Materials primarily in English, with issues of Brooklyn Berichten in Dutch, issues of FAC News, The Fifth Avenue Press, and InFACt in both English and Spanish, and issues of Nordic News in both English and Norwegian.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original issues from Volumes 1 and 2 of Guy Pierrepont's Weekly Society Bulletin are in very fragile condition and should not be used unless the provided preservation photocopies will not suffice for the researcher's purpose.
Otherwise this collection is open to researchers without restriction.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Source and date of acquisition for many items in the collection are unknown. Where the items' source(s) are known, that information is indicated at the file level. Many were evidently sent to the Brooklyn Historical Society or Brooklyn Public Library directly.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: If digital surrogates exist, they should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Brooklyn newsletter collection, ARC.174, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Originally minimally processed to the collection level, this collection was reprocessed to the folder level in 2022. The archivist discovered that many of the materials in the collection could not strictly be called newsletters and removed them to more appropriate composite collections such as the Brooklyn Historical Society ephemera collection (ARC.272) and Brooklyn Playbills and Programs (BCMS.0041). In addition, newsletters related to schools were removed to the Brooklyn schools collection (CBHM.0006). Newsletters from both the Brooklyn Historical Society ephemera collection (ARC.272) and the Brooklyn ephemera collection (BCMS.0007) have been incorporated into this collection.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Brooklyn neighborhood associations and civic organizations publications (ARC.167)
Carlisle Indian: The Newsletter of the Carlisle Club (BCMS.0024)
Linewaiters' Gazette Collection (BCMS.0027)

Materials from the various organizations and businesses represented here can also be found in the Brooklyn ephemera collection (BCMS.0007) and the Brooklyn Historical Society ephemera collection (ARC.272).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Brooklyn newsletter collection contains newsletters from Brooklyn-based businesses and organizations. The newsletters vary in length and quality and touch on a variety of different subjects including Brooklyn neighborhoods, housing, economic issues, and community updates.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: While many items at the Center for Brooklyn History are unrestricted, we do not own reproduction rights to all materials. Be aware of the several kinds of rights that might apply: copyright, licensing and trademarks. The researcher assumes all responsibility for copyright questions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. 1-3. Published quarterly by the Society for the Historical Preservation of the Fourteenth Brooklyn State Militia Infantry (84th New York Infantry) Incorporated.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also The Red Legged Devil.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 30 No. 3. Published by Abraham & Straus.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Abraham & Straus collection (ARC.223)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Greensward Foundation, Inc., "the friends of Central Park, Prospect Park, Fort Greene Park, Branch Brook Park, Druid Hill Park and Cadwalader Park."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. V No. 2. Published by the Green-Wood Historic Fund of Green-Wood Cemetery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Number 15 (Vol. V No. 3). A quarterly publication of the Ascent Institute: Inner dimensions of Jewish life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 9 No. 1; Vol. 11 No. 4. Astella Development Corporation, Coney Island.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1 No. 1. Arts and culture magazine published by Malcolm Smart. Includes AIDS Watch column.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. I No. 3. Published by the Avonian Dramatic Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published seasonally by the Brooklyn Alliance of Neighborhood Gardens (BANG).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. V-X. Published by the Brooklyn Economic Development Corporation.

did.unittitle: The Bedford Stuyvesant Little League News

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. IX Nos. 1 and 2. "Published as a Service to the Youth of the Community by the Breevort Savings Bank."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dignity/Brooklyn, local chapter of Dignity/USA. "The newsletter of Brooklyn's caring community of lesbian and gay Roman Catholics and their friends." Includes some Dignity/USA newsletters also.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Piet E. Schreuders, Managing Editor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Holland by Ed Schilders and Piet Schreuders.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 25 No. 4. Published monthly by the Brooklyn chapter of the National Association of Accountants (NAA).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nos. 1-25. Published by Metro Sports Look, Inc., NJ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. 1-5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. XXVII No. 8. Published monthly by the Junior League of Brooklyn, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Borough President Howard Golden's Brooklyn newsletter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by the Better Brooklyn Committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 2 No. 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A publication of the Brooklyn Union Gas Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1 No. 1. Published quarterly by the Brooklyn branch of the New York Urban League.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 8 Nos. 2-3 and Vol. IX No. 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 4 No. 1. Published by CAMBA (Church Avenue Merchant Block Association), based in Flatbush.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by the Prospect Park Environmental Center (est. 1978), which became the Brooklyn Center for the Urban Environment (BCUE) in 1990.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Brooklyn Center for the Urban Environment collection (BCMS.0066)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Community Board #9.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1 Nos. 1, 3, 7. Published by Coney Island, USA.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. XI No. 25. "The Voice of Brooklyn Scouts" (Boy Scouts).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Terry Lawler, Prudential Douglas Elliman, Park Slope.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Some issues were the gift of Clark Marlor and some were the gift of Terry Lawler.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 6 No. 1. Published by and primarily focuses on the Los Angeles Dodgers but with some information about the Brooklyn Dodgers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A quarterly newsletter brought to you by the three Downtown Brooklyn Business Improvement Districts (BIDs) and the Downtown Brooklyn Partnership."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nos. 21, 23, 24, 28, 29. Published by the Brooklyn Center at Long Island University.

relatedmaterial.p: See also Long Island University News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. XXIV-XXVI

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Citizens for a Better New York.

did.langmaterial: This publication is in both English and Spanish.

did.langmaterial: This publication is in both English and Spanish.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. 1-7. Published and edited by Norman E. Kurland and seems to be related to the Brooklyn Dodgers Baseball Hall of Fame.

scopecontent.p: Vol. 6 No. 6. Published by G.F.N. Gay Friends and Neighbors Ltd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. III Nos. 3 and 11. Published by Flare Corp., "the one stop print shop," Nostrand Avenue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 3 No. 1. "The Gowanus Arts Exchange is the home of Brooklyn's Center for Lesbian and Gay Arts and Artists, and of the Brooklyn Dance Consortium."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1. Published by the Parks and Recreation Department/Brooklyn.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Vols. 1-2 (1941-1942); Vol. 3 (1963-1964). Volume 1 billed as a "supplement to the 1940 Brooklyn Heights Blue Book."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original issues from Volumes 1 and 2 are in very fragile condition and should not be used unless the provided preservation photocopies will not suffice for the researcher's purpose.

did.langmaterial: This publication is in both English and Spanish.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. VII-XI. Published by the Kings County Grand Jurors' Association.

scopecontent.p: Issues 41, 46, 50, 52-54, 56, 57. "The Weekly Publication for every Jewish Person." Published by Lubavitch Youth Organization.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published quarterly by the Long Island Empire Chapter, Telephone Pioneers of America, Brooklyn.

scopecontent.p: "Official publication of the New York Navy Yard, Brooklyn, New York [Brooklyn Navy Yard]. Distributed free to all Yard employees."

Microfilm 1941-1966

did.langmaterial: This publication is in both English and Norwegian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The quarterly newsletter of the Scandinavian East Coast Museum.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Another publication with the same title is available on microfilm. See the Local newspapers on microfilm collection (BCMS.0028).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issues from Vols. 7-43. Published by George W. Pease and Son, Inc., funeral directors, Nostrand Avenue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "A newsletter for members and friends of the Old Stone House."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1 Nos. 1-2, Vol. 2 No. 3. Published by the Paragon Progressive Federal Credit Union and Paragon Progressive Community Association, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Nos. 15-17. Published by Positive Focus, Inc., a nonprofit center for emerging photoartists.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: The collection Real Brooklyn, a day in our lives photographs (2007.041) documents a 2003 Positive Focus exhibition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seasonal guides published by the Park with grant assistance and later by the Prospect Park Alliance after its founding in April 1987.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also V.I.P.P. Newsletter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 6 No. 2. The newsletter of the Society for the Historical Preservation of the Brooklyn State Militia Infantry.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also 14th Brooklyn Society News.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. 14-15. Published by the Douglas MacArthur Republican Club, Brooklyn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Employee publication at Brooklyn Union (formerly Brooklyn Union Gas).

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Brooklyn Union's New York, Gas Works, Starline.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. XVI No. 6. A publication for employees of Securities Industry Automation Corporation, MetroTech Center, Brooklyn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 9 No. 3. Published by the Flatbush Development Corporation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Augustana Lutheran Home, Bensonhurst.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 2 No. 6 and Vol. 5 No. 4. Published quarterly by the Flatbush Historical Society and Town of Flatbush (Civic and) Cultural Association, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vols. 1-2. "A paper to be published regularly by Brooklyn College Staff. It is a political paper, exressing the program of the Communist Party, with especial reference to the problems of teachers and students in Brooklyn College."
The first issue's masthead reads "Brooklyn College Staff" and Vol. 2 No. 3 reads "Student and Staff." All other issues in the collection are titled "The Staff."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Brooklyn Union Gas.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Brooklyn Union's New York, Sendout, and Gas Works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1 No. 3. This publication is not Brooklyn-specific but the bulk of this issue is devoted to reprinting a 1906 article from the official publication of the Brooklyn Rapid Transit Employees' Benefit Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 8 No. 5. Published by Machne Israel, Inc. Tzvios HaShem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Neighborhood Open Space Coalition/Friends of Gateway.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Gateway.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published for Volunteers in Prospect Park.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Prospect Park.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A publication of the Washington Avenue-Prospect Heights Association.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1 Nos. 1-6. Published by the Home Title Insurance Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "The Newspaper of Project Reach Youth"'s journalism program Project Express. "Written and developed by teens from Park Slope and Sunset Park."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Community Y.M. and Y.W.H.A. (Young Men's and Young Women's Hebrew Association), Brownsville.
